info.title: Sentinel Web Services (SWERVE)
info.description:
Sentinel Web Services

The purpose of this document is to define the interfaces between Sentinel and customer systems. This definition will be used to develop software changes at Quorum and the customer.

post.description:
The EDL MEL Submission Interface will allow the customer’s system to send a MEL submission(s) to Sentinel for submission to National Grid via EDL.

Customer will call this method to invoke an EDL MEL Submission containing the posted data. If the MELs were successfully submitted the web service will respond with the reference numbers of the submitted MELs.

In order for Sentinel to successfully submit an EDL message the message must conform to National Grid’s validation rules. Therefore Sentinel will validate the EDL MEL submission data using National Grid’s rules before it is submitted. The validation rules for EDL MEL submissions can be found at https://www.nationalgrid.com/sites/default/files/documents/32071-DVCD%20Rules%20v9.pdf Section 3.6 Valid Maximum Export Limits. The EDL MEL Submission Interface will return validation messages if any values in the EDL MEL submission data does not conform to these validation rules.

A MEL submission post may contain a number of separate MEL submissions and therefore the results may contain both successful and failed submissions, this is indicated by the Status and ValidationMessage fields in the returned json.

Sentinel will only be able to make an EDL Submission if there is a verified connection between Sentinel and the National Grid BM EDL System. The EDL MEL Submission Interface will return a 502 Bad Gateway error if there is an attempt to make a submission when there is no verified connection.

The EDL MEL Submission Interface will return a 400 Bad Request error if there is an attempt to make a submission with invalid json.

National Grid have built the National Grid BM EDL System assuming the EDL Messages are generated from a user using a software client. Therefore the National Grid BM EDL System cannot handle high volumes of data. Typical message volumes would be up to 10 messages per BM Unit per half hour. National grid receive each dynamic data point or ramp segment as single message so grouping multiple data points into one API call does not reduce the number of submissions sent to national grid.

requestBody.description: EDL MEL object that needs to be submitted

post.description:
The EDL MIL Submission Interface will allow the customer’s systems to send a MIL submission to Sentinel for submission to National Grid via EDL.

Customer will call this method to invoke an EDL MIL Submission containing the posted data. If the MILs were successfully submitted the web service will respond with the reference numbers of the submitted MILs.

In order for Sentinel to successfully submit an EDL message the message must conform to National Grid’s validation rules. Therefore Sentinel will validate the EDL MIL submission data using National Grid’s rules before it is submitted. The validation rules for EDL MIL submissions can be found at https://www.nationalgrid.com/sites/default/files/documents/32071-DVCD%20Rules%20v9.pd Section 3.7 Valid Maximum Import Limits. The EDL MILSubmission Interface will return validation messages if any values in the EDL MIL submission data does not conform to these validation rules.

A MIL submission post may contain a number of separate MIL submissions and therefore the results may contain both successful and failed submissions, this is indicated by the Status and ValidationMessage fields in the returned json.

Sentinel will only be able to make an EDL Submission if there is a verified connection between Sentinel and the National Grid BM EDL System. The EDL MIL Submission Interface will return a 502 Bad Gateway error if there is an attempt to make a submission when there is no verified connection.

The EDL MIL Submission Interface will return a 400 Bad Request error if there is an attempt to make a submission with invalid json.

National Grid have built the National Grid BM EDL System assuming the EDL Messages are generated from a user using a software client. Therefore the National Grid BM EDL System cannot handle high volumes of data. Typical message volumes would be up to 10 messages per BM Unit per half hour. National grid receive each dynamic data point or ramp segment as single message so grouping multiple data points into one API call does not reduce the number of submissions sent to national grid.

requestBody.description: EDL MIL object that needs to be submitted

post.description:
The customer’s system require Dynamic Data for a BM Unit to be submitted by Sentinel to National Grid. Dynamic data is used by National Grid to define the parameters within which bid offer acceptances can be issued. Dynamic data is effective from the time it is accepted by National Grid. The EDL Dynamic Data Submission Interface will allow the customer’s systems to send Dynamic Data submissions to Sentinel for onward submission to National Grid via EDL.

Customer will call this method to invoke an EDL Dynamic Data Submission containing the posted data types. If the Dynamic Data types were successfully submitted the web service will respond with the reference numbers of the submitted data types.

In order for Sentinel to successfully submit an EDL message the message must conform to National Grid’s validation rules. Therefore Sentinel will validate the EDL Dynamic Data submission using National Grid’s rules before it is submitted. The validation rules for EDL Dynamic Data submissions can be found at https://www.nationalgrid.com/sites/default/files/documents/32071-DVCD%20Rules%20v9.pdf in the following sections:

  • 3.8 Valid Run-up and Run-down Rates
  • 3.9 Valid Notice to Deviate from Zero
  • 3.10 Valid Notice to Deliver Offers
  • 3.11 Valid Notice to Deliver Bids
  • 3.12 Valid Minimum Zero Time
  • 3.13 Valid Minimum Non-Zero Time
  • 3.14 Valid Stable Export Limit
  • 3.15 Valid Stable Import Limit
  • 3.16 Valid Maximum Delivery Volume
  • 3.17 Valid Maximum Delivery Period

The EDL Dynamic Data Submission Interface will return validation messages if any values in the EDL MEL submission data does not conform to these validation rules.

A Dynamic Data Submission post may contain a number of separate Dynamic Data submissions and therefore the results may contain both successful and failed submissions, this is indicated by the Status and ValidationMessage fields in the returned json. However it is recommended that you only submit changed data via the API as NAtional Grid systems are sensative to the amount of messages received. If only one dynamic parameter has changed then please only submit that one data point rather than all dynamics.

Sentinel will only be able to make an EDL Submission if there is a verified connection between Sentinel and the National Grid BM EDL System. The EDL Dynamic Data Submission Interface will return a 502 Bad Gateway error if there is an attempt to make a submission when there is no verified connection.

The EDL Dynamic Data Submission Interface will return a 400 Bad Request error if there is an attempt to make a submission with invalid json.

National Grid have built the National Grid BM EDL System assuming the EDL Messages are generated from a user using a software client. Therefore the National Grid BM EDL System cannot handle high volumes of data. Typical message volumes would be up to 10 messages per BM Unit per half hour. National grid receive each dynamic data point or ramp segment as single message so grouping multiple data points into one API call does not reduce the number of submissions sent to national grid.

Customer require the ability to submit PN, MEL, MIL and BOD data to National Grid via EDT. The EDT Submission interface will allow the customer’s system to send PN/MEL/MIL/BOD submissions to Sentinel EDT. Sentinel EDT will validate the contents of the submission to Sentinel and if the contents are valid a submission will be sent to National Grid’s BM EDT system.

National Grid EDT data must conform to the validation rules defined in https://www.nationalgrid.com/sites/default/files/documents/32071-DVCD%20Rules%20v9.pdf.

In most cases a single EDT Submission web service call will result in one file being submitted to National Grid. However if tolerance rules are enabled and configured then multiple submissions could be generated from a single web service call. The response from the EDT Submission web service call will include the negative SWERVE ID reference number(s) of the generated import files. The customer’s web client can then query the EDT Submission status web service to determine the status of each import, or corresponding submission, file.

Response will have a status of one of the following: Processed, Failed, Timed out, Validation failed

Either Elexon BM Unit Id or NGC BM Unit Id are valid inputs inputs to the 'UnitId' field.

As the NGC EDT submission process can take a long time the status returned only indicates if the submission has been processed and not whether this has been accepted or rejected by NGC. The end point /sentinel/edt/submissions/get-submission should be called using the referance number returned from this and point as the input to that one. This should then be called once per minute for a maximum of 20 minutes at which point the submission should hit a final status.

The possible EDT submission status are as follows:

  • inserting
  • inserted
  • processing
  • processed
  • failed
  • validationFailed
  • timedout
  • cancelled
  • active
  • sent
  • submitted
  • pending

requestBody.description:
EDT Data for submission.

Please note that due to National Grid validation rules data for Bid Offer Pairs 1 and -1 must be provided as a minimum when submitting BOD data.

post.description:
This endpoint is a generic interface used to submit Time Series data into Sentinel, this is a time and value array. The values are for a discrete granularity.

This feeds into our configurable data dictionary. As part of the call a combination of GUID and DataTag is used to determine which data item should be written to.

As part of each implementation the details of the GUIDs, DataTags and granularity will be communicated separately.

Each data item can be configured to be of any granularity. The data provided needs to be contiguous and match the configured granularity. The current implementation also expects the data to start on a half hour boundary.

This end point will return a reference number if the JSON is schematically valid.

The end point /sentinel-interface-gateway/Services/getSubmissionStatus should then be called to establish if the writing of the data was successful.

Used to submit generic Single Value into Sentinel. This end point will retrun a reference number if the JSON is schematically valid.

This endpoint is a generic interface used to submit Single Value data into Sentinel, this is a time from and value array. The values are evergreen and are effective until the next time ordered value.

This feeds into our configurable data dictionary. As part of the call a combination of GUID and DataTag is used to determine which data item should be written to.

As part of each implementation the details of the GUIDs and DataTags will be communicated separately.

The data provided will be merged into the existing values in the data item.

This end point will return a reference number if the JSON is schematically valid.

The end point /sentinel-interface-gateway/Services/getSubmissionStatus should then be called to establish if the writing of the data was successful.

This endpoint is a generic interface used to submit Ramp Data into Sentinel, this is a time from, value and time to, value array. The values are for a defined time range.

This feeds into our configurable data dictionary. As part of the call a combination of GUID and DataTag is used to determine which data item should be written to.

As part of each implementation the details of the GUIDs and DataTags will be communicated separately.

The data provided needs to be contiguous and time from must be earlier than time to.

This end point will return a reference number if the JSON is schematically valid.

The end point /sentinel-interface-gateway/Services/getSubmissionStatus should then be called to establish if the writing of the data was successful.

get.description:
This is a interface to return feedback of submissions made for the following endpoints; submitSingleValue, submitTimeSeries and submitRampData.

The GUID, Name and data granularity are all validated as part of this write. If any issues occurs and the data is not written then these errors are returned here.

parameters.name: referenceNo
parameters.description: Reference Number received during submission

200.description: Submission Status Information - If a reader-friendly name has been mapped to the GUID then this will be displayed in the 'Name' field. Possible status responses - Created, Submitted, Acknowledged, Accepted, Rejected, Failed, Timed Out. AuditId is a unique numerical identifier of the data posted under the data tag in question, assigned by Sentinel.

example.message: Error message on the server side

post.description:
This method sets the AutoBOA status for a station.

See the new expanded Set EDL Auto Instruction Options endpoint.

parameters.name: UnitId
parameters.description: The name of the BM Unit to apply status to.

parameters.name: Status
parameters.description: The boolean status to be applied.

get.description:
This method gets the current AutoBOA status for a station.

See the new expanded Get EDL Auto Instruction Options endpoint.

parameters.name: UnitId
parameters.description: The name of the BM Unit to retrieve status for.

post.description:
This method sets the EDL Auto Instruction Options for a station.

Any fields that are not included in the request body won't be changed.

Note that the minutes integers must be greater than or equal to 0 and the action strings are case insensitive enums (see schema).

This method returns all fields, not just those included in the request body. This is equivalent to calling Get EDL Auto Instruction Options straight after this method.

requestBody.description: The EDL Auto Instruction Options to be set.

get.description:
This method gets the current EDL Auto Instruction Options for a station.

Note that PumpedStorage will return "Unavailable" if the system is not configured for pumped storage instructions

parameters.name: unit
parameters.description: The name of the BM Unit to retrieve options for.

post.description: This method sets the BOP enabled flag for a unit.

parameters.name: unit
parameters.description: The name of the BM Unit to set the flag for.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: The BOP enabled flag to be set.

get.description: This method gets the BOP enabled flag for a unit.

parameters.name: unit
parameters.description: The name of the BM Unit to get the flag for.

get.description:
This method will obtain data item data for the supplied parameters in a time series fashion, such that a date and value list is created.

Note: This method will retrieve a maximum 4320 total data points – this is three days at one minute granularity.

parameters.name: dataItem
parameters.description: The name of the Data Item to retrieve.

parameters.name: stationName
parameters.description: The Station name in which the Data Item shall be retrieved from.

parameters.name: datefrom
parameters.description:
The date and time of when to retrieve the data from.
This should be in the format of yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.

parameters.name: dateto
parameters.description:
The date and time of when to retrieve the data to.
This should be in the format of yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.

parameters.name: snapshotTime
parameters.description:
The date time of the snapshot to retrieve.
This should be in the format of yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss
Defaults to now.

parameters.name: granularity
parameters.description: The granularity of the Data Item in seconds.

parameters.name: site
parameters.description: The site in which the Data Item shall be retrieved from. If site is present it will take priority over stationName.

Note: This method will retrieve a maximum 4320 total data points – this is three days at one minute granularity.

parameters.name: dataItem

parameters.name: stationName

parameters.name: snapshotTime

This should be in the format of yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss

parameters.name: site
parameters.description: The site in which the Data Item shall be retrieved from. If site is present it will take priority over stationName.

get.description: This method will obtain data item data for the supplied parameters in a ramp data fashion, which will consist of time from, time to, level from, level to and type.

parameters.name: dataItem
parameters.description: The name of the Data Item to retrieve.

parameters.name: stationName
parameters.description: The Station name in which the Data Item shall be retrieved from.

parameters.name: datefrom
parameters.description:
The date and time of when to retrieve the data from.
This should be in the format of yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.

parameters.name: dateto

The date and time of when to retrieve the data to.
This should be in the format of yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.

parameters.name: snapshotTime
parameters.description:
The date time of the snapshot to retrieve.
This should be in the format of yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss
Defaults to now.

parameters.name: site
parameters.description: The site in which the Data Item shall be retrieved from. If site is present it will take priority over stationName.

get.description: This method will return the station list.
This method will return the station block list.
This method will return a list of all data items grouped by type.

This method gets the EDL SP Position data for the specified parameters.  For MW profiles listed (PN, BOA PN, MEL, MIL, SEL, SIL) the SP Position is determined as the equivalent constant MW value necessary to produce the same area under the graph as the actual ramped profile between the SP boundaries.

parameters.name: unit
parameters.description: The name of the generating unit. Specify “All” to return all BM Unit data or a comma separated list of individual names for example “BMU-1,BMU-2”.

parameters.name: datatype
parameters.description:
The data type to retrieve for. This can be:

  • All
  • PN
  • BOA PN
  • MEL
  • MIL
  • SEL
  • SIL
  • NDZ
  • NTO
  • NTB
  • MZT
  • MNZT
  • MDV
  • MDP
  • MDVP
  • MDP
  • Target MVAR

The values can also be specified as a comma separated list for example “PN,MEL”.

parameters.name: datefrom
parameters.description:
The date and time of when to retrieve the data from. This should be in the format of yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss
NOTE: The value should be on a SP boundary. If it is not then the date will be rounded down to the previous SP boundary.

parameters.name: dateto
parameters.description:
The date and time of when to retrieve the data from. This should be in the format of yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss
NOTE: The value should be on a SP boundary. If it is not then the date will be rounded down to the previous SP boundary.

parameters.name: site
parameters.description: The site whose database to use - needs to be configured per customer

get.description: This method gets the EDL Spot Value data for a single time value using the specified parameters.

parameters.name: unit
parameters.description: The name of the generating unit. Specify “All” to return all BM Unit data or a comma separated list of individual names for example “BMU-1,BMU-2”.

parameters.name: datatype
parameters.description:
The data type to retrieve for. This can be:

  • All
  • PN
  • BOA PN
  • MEL
  • MIL
  • SEL
  • SIL
  • NDZ
  • NTO
  • NTB
  • MZT
  • MNZT
  • MDV
  • MDP
  • MDVP
  • MDP
  • Target MVAR
  • RURE
  • RURI
  • RDRE
  • RDRI

The values can also be specified as a comma separated list for example “PN,MEL”.

parameters.name: datefrom
parameters.description: The date and time of when to retrieve the data from.  This should be in the format of yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.

get.description:
This method gets the EDL Profile Value breakpoints between the time values specified for the specified BM units.

Note Breakpoints will be calculated at the start and end times used in the query if they do not exist in the database. Breakpoints will also be added at the settlement period boundaries for BOA PN which will also be at integer level. When ramping through a settlement period boundary this may result in a slight kink in the ramp due to rounding.

For MEL data type the accept field will be included in the response. This indicates if the portion of the MEL has been submitted to NESO (true) or if it is part of the future MEL profile that has not yet been submitted (false).

parameters.name: unit
parameters.description: The name of the generating unit. Specify “All” to return all BM Unit data or a comma separated list of individual names for example “BMU-1,BMU-2”.

parameters.name: datatype
parameters.description:
The data type to retrieve for. This can be:

  • All
  • PN
  • BOA PN
  • MEL
  • MIL
  • SEL
  • SIL
  • NDZ
  • NTO
  • NTB
  • MZT
  • MNZT
  • MDV
  • MDP
  • MDVP
  • MDP
  • Target MVAR
  • RURE
  • RURI
  • RDRE
  • RDRI
  • Target Freq
  • BOA
  • Public MEL
  • Public CCL

The values can also be specified as a comma separated list for example “PN,MEL”.

parameters.name: datefrom
parameters.description: The date and time of when to retrieve the data from.  This should be in the format of yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.

parameters.name: dateto
parameters.description: The date and time of when to retrieve the data from.  This should be in the format of yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.

parameters.name: includereasoncode
parameters.description:
This is either:

  • true
  • false

If true then the returned data will include the loss code and the reason code in the response.If not included they will be left out.

get.description: This method gets the Snapshot Ramp Profile between the effective time values specified for the specified BM unit.

parameters.name: unit
parameters.description: The name of the generating unit. Can only supply one unit at a time.

parameters.name: datatype
parameters.description:
The data type to retrieve for. This can be:

  • MEL
  • MIL

parameters.name: effectiveDateFrom
parameters.description: The date and time of when to retrieve the data from.  This should be in the format of yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.

parameters.name: effectiveDateTo
parameters.description: The date and time of when to retrieve the data from.  This should be in the format of yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.

parameters.name: snapshotTime
parameters.description:
The date and time the snapshot to use.
This should be in the format of yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.

get.description: This method gets the Snapshot Settlement Period Average between the effective time values specified for the specified BM unit.

parameters.name: unit
parameters.description: The name of the generating unit. Can only supply one unit at a time.

parameters.name: datatype
parameters.description:
The data type to retrieve for. This can be:

  • MEL
  • MIL

parameters.name: effectiveDateFrom
parameters.description: The date and time of when to retrieve the data from.  This should be in the format of yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.

parameters.name: effectiveDateTo
parameters.description: The date and time of when to retrieve the data from.  This should be in the format of yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.

parameters.name: snapshotTime
parameters.description:
The date and time the snapshot to use.
This should be in the format of yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.

get.description: This method gets the EDL Submission data for the specified parameters. This data comprises both the header data common to all EDL submissions e.g. type, reference number, logged time, accepted time etc. and also the message details specific to that type of submission.

parameters.name: unit
parameters.description:
The name of the generating unit.
Specify “All” to return all BM Unit data or a comma separated list of individual names for example “BMU-1,BMU-2”.

parameters.name: datatype
parameters.description:
This can be either:

  • All
  • MEL
  • MIL
  • SEL
  • SIL
  • RURE
  • RDRE
  • RURI
  • RDRI
  • NTO
  • NTB
  • NDZ
  • MZT
  • MNZT
  • MDV
  • MDP

The values can also be specified as a comma separated list for example “MEL,MIL”.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
This can be either:

  • All
  • Accepted
  • Rejected
  • Tech Ack
  • Acknowledged

parameters.name: datefrom
parameters.description:
The date and time of when to retrieve the data from. This should be in the format of yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss
NOTE: This is based on the log time of the instruction.

parameters.name: dateto
parameters.description:
The date and time of when to retrieve the data from. This should be in the format of yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss
NOTE: This is based on the log time of the instruction.

parameters.name: includetelephone
parameters.description:
This is either:

  • true
  • false

If true then telephone instructions will be included otherwise false.

parameters.name: includedata
parameters.description:
This is either:

  • true
  • false

If true then submission data will be included in the response, otherwise just the edl submission header data will be returned.

parameters.name: includeAdditionalDetails
parameters.description:
This is either:

  • true
  • false

If true then additional plant maintenance code data if available will be included in the response, otherwise just the edl submission header data will be returned. This will only appear for MEL submissions and only if this functionality is active in Sentinel

get.description: This method gets the EDL Submissions status for the specified parameters.

parameters.name: referenceNo
parameters.description: The reference number to query. Can be a single reference number or a comma separated list.

parameters.name: station
parameters.description: This is used to specify which Station database to use to retrieve EDL data when in a multi-station setup. By default this goes to the EMC(or data centre) database.

get.description: This method gets the EDL Instruction data for the specified parameters. This data comprises both the header data common to all EDL instructions e.g. type, reference number, logged time, accepted time etc. and also the message details specific to that type of instruction.

parameters.name: unit
parameters.description: The name of the generating unit. Specify “All” to return all BM Unit data or a comma separated list of individual names for example “BMU-1,BMU-2”.

parameters.name: datatype
parameters.description:
The data type to retrieve for. This can be:

  • All
  • BOAI
  • REAS
  • PUMP/PUMPED_STORAGE/PUMPED STORAGE
  • STATUS
  • VOLT
  • MVAR
  • Target Freq

The values can also be specified as a comma separated list for example “BOA,REAS”.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
The status of the instruction. This can be:

  • Accepted
  • Rejected
  • Acknowledged
  • All

If the status is not specified this is defaulted to Accepted.

parameters.name: datefrom
parameters.description:
The date and time of when to retrieve the data from. This should be in the format of yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss
NOTE: This is based on the log time of the instruction.

parameters.name: dateto
parameters.description:
The date and time of when to retrieve the data from. This should be in the format of yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss
NOTE: This is based on the log time of the instruction.

parameters.name: includetelephone
parameters.description:
This is either:

  • true
  • false

If true then telephone instructions will be included otherwise false.

parameters.name: includeeffective
parameters.description:
This is either:

  • true
  • false

When effective time is set to true

  • For discrete values, only the values selected within the specified time range are displayed.
  • For evergreen values, all active values during the time range are displayed, even if they fall outside the specified range, as these values are considered evergreen and remain active.

When effective time is set to false

  • Only the values within the specified range of the get-data are displayed, regardless of whether they are discrete or evergreen.

get.description:
This method gets the EDT Profile Value breakpoints between the time values specified for the specified BM units.

Note Breakpoints will be calculated at the start and end times used in the query if they do not exist in the database. Breakpoints will also be added at the settlement period boundaries for BOA PN which will also be at integer level. When ramping through a settlement period boundary this may result in a slight kink in the ramp due to rounding.

parameters.name: unit
parameters.description: The name of the generating unit. Specify “All” to return all BM Unit data or a comma separated list of individual names for example “BMU-1,BMU-2”.

parameters.name: datatype
parameters.description:
The data type to retrieve for. This can be:

  • All
  • PN
  • BOA PN
  • MEL
  • MIL
  • SEL
  • SIL
  • NDZ
  • NTO
  • NTB
  • MZT
  • MNZT
  • MDV
  • MDP
  • MDVP
  • MDP
  • RURE
  • RURI
  • RDRE
  • RDRI

The values can also be specified as a comma separated list for example “PN,MEL”.

parameters.name: datefrom
parameters.description: The date and time of when to retrieve the data from. This should be in the format of yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss

parameters.name: dateto
parameters.description: The date and time of when to retrieve the data from. This should be in the format of yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss

get.description:
Bid Offer data has a specialized data structure where the values can only change on the half hour boundary (and hold that value for the next 30 minutes). They also have multiple values for the same time period (level 1, bid price 1, offer price 1 etc.). For this reason, the SP position is the same as the spot value for a half hour. The profile position is also the same as the SP position over a range of SPs.
This method gets the EDT Bid Offer Position data for the specified parameters.

parameters.name: unit
parameters.description: The name of the generating unit. Specify “All” to return all BM Unit data or a comma separated list of individual names for example “BMU-1,BMU-2”.

parameters.name: datefrom
parameters.description:
The date and time of when to retrieve the data from. This should be in the format of yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss
NOTE: The value should be on a SP boundary. If it is not then the date will be rounded down to the previous SP boundary.

parameters.name: dateto
parameters.description:
The date and time of when to retrieve the data from. This should be in the format of yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss
NOTE: The value should be on a SP boundary. If it is not then the date will be rounded down to the previous SP boundary.

get.description:
This interface allows the customer system to obtain EDT Submission details and status with National Grid, for the EDT submission assigned the Reference ID that is being queried by the customer.

As the NGC EDT submission process can take a long time the status the submission end point only indicates if the import has been processed and not whether the corresponding submission has been accepted or rejected by NGC. This end point can therefore be called using the reference number returned from the submission end point as the input. This should then be called once per minute for a maximum of 20 minutes at which point the submission should hit a final status. If a file is rejected then an internal warning should be raised for the end user to open up the Sentinel EDT Client to inspect the reasons for the rejection.

The possible EDT submission status are as follows:

  • <no submission> (message: ["Imported, submission pending"])
  • Pending Submission
  • Created
  • Submitted To NGC
  • ACK
  • Accepted
    • This is a Final Status
  • Rejected
    • This is a Final Status
  • Accepted & Rejected
    • This is a Final Status
  • Failed
    • This is a Final Status

parameters.name: referenceNo
parameters.description: Reference Number received during submission

200.description: Submission Data. If a datetime field is not applicable, e.g. "rejectTime" when submission has been accepted, then a value of null is returned.

example.message: Error message on the server side

post.description:
This method is used to submit an event and mimics the functionality from the THOR client for adding/editing events.

Once the a valid post request has been processed by the server it will return an ReferenceNumber back. This can be used to either edit the event later by providing InstanceID in the post request or to view the event via the get-event-status endpoint.

Data can be used to send single or multi time series data (one piece of data being single, more than one is multi) Cancelling an event can be done by sending "Cancelled" in the EventStatus field and providing a valid InstanceID Remit Reasons and Event Categories can be viewed using the get-remit-reasons and get-event-categories endpoints.

get.description: This method gets the credit cover data imported from the E014 file.

parameters.name: bscParty
parameters.description: The BSC Party

parameters.name: settlementDate
parameters.description: The settlement date for which the data applies.

parameters.name: ecvaaNo
parameters.description: The ECVAA Number

get.summary: Return a list of distinct BSC parties in the system
Pass in date and return all ID's, BSC parties and run types/run numbers. Any values that are null will be excluded from the response automatically.

Pass in SAA I014 ID and return all data. Can include or exclude tables and columns. Any values that are null will be excluded from the response automatically. Cannot have include and exclude in the same request. Any table or column can be used for the include or exclude. If you wish to include or exclude multiple tables, you use '~'. If you want to specify specific columns, put them in a set of () and seperate them with ','.

Pass in date and bsc party and/or runType and runNumber and return a report from the ReportHeader table. Passing in just a Date and Bsc Party will return a report for the latest runType. Passing a RunType as well will return a report with the highest runNumber. Passing in a runNumber as well will return the report with that specific runNumber. Any values that are null will be excluded from the response automatically.

post.description:
As the Elexon submission process can take a long time the id returned should be used to then determine when the final status of the submisson. The end point /sentinel/elexon/imports/get-status should be called using the referance number returned. This should then be called once per minute for a maximum of 20 minutes at which point the submission should hit a final status.

When posting trade or position data via the API the Reference Code is important, as it will determine how Elexon will treat any subsequent notification.

If operating in 'Additional' mode then a unique Reference Code should be used for each individual trade. Elexon will then add the volumes to the relevant Energy Account balance. If operating in 'Replacement' mode then it is expected that the data is net position rather than individual trade data, and the data should cover a single Settlement Day only with a Reference Code relating to that day. The suggested format for such a Reference Code is 'DDMMYY', and this can be used for multiple ECVNAAs. Once a Reference Code has been used, Elexon will will treat any subsequent use of the same Reference Code for a particular ECVNAA as meaning the ECVN contains the latest data and so, rather than add the data to the relevant Energy Account Balance, will replace existing period values with the latest submitted ones. For this reason, care should be taken to always include the whole Settlement Day in any submission, to avoid the circumstance in which omitted data is treated as, effectively, a zero position.

Any trade volumes should be represented as no more than 3 decimal places due to Elexon limits on the ECVN submissions.

get.description:
As the Elexon submission process can take a long time the id returned should be used to then determine when the final status of the import. This should then be called once per minute for a maximum of 20 minutes at which point the submission should hit a final status.

A single import may result in one or many ECVN submissions depending on the time period it is covering. So an array of ECVN submissions will be returned form this end point. It is also possible that an import could result in no ECVN submission being made in which case only the import details will be returned.

parameters.name: ReferenceNo
parameters.description: Reference Number received during submission

200.description: Submission Data - One of the following Status values will be returned - Inserted,Pending,Sent,Processed,Rejected,Failed,Cancelled.

post.description: This endpoint is used to save and submit baskets to an Auction date(s) and edit existing baskets. Either all baskets in a submission will be successfully submitted to SOLV, or all baskets will fail. When submitting a new basket, it is important to not include any basket identifier, like solvId or externalId, because a new basket will be created. However, if you are editing an existing basket, you must provide either a solvId or an externalId for that basket.

EAC Submit Baskets

Note that submission.auctionType must always be set to "Response"
